candidate_summary: Yoruba Ifá divination-inspired LLM evaluator: 8-bit binary signature from 8-axis evaluation (factuality, fluency, harm, reasoning, tone, format, constraint, citation) maps to 256-verse pre-built corpus with rubric-interpretation, canonical example, and ritual repair plan; 8-round palm-nut protocol with 16-judge cohort per axis; babalawo-interpreter LLM consumes (signature, output, corpus_entry) for final assessment; 16x16 compositional rubric tree (main-Odu x sub-Odu).
content_words: yoruba ifa odu palm-nut babalawo divination 16
ikin palm-nut count remaining transfer ritual
ojú odù amúlù iyanifa main combined
8-bit binary signature 8-axis evaluation factuality fluency harm reasoning tone format constraint citation
256-verse pre-built corpus lookup rubric-interpretation + canonical example + ritual repair plan per signature
8-round palm-nut sample protocol 16-judge cohort tau-threshold binary axis derivation
babalawo-interpreter LLM + 16x16 compositional rubric tree main-sub Odu decomposition explanation

results.name: Autorubric Unifying Rubric-Based LLM Evaluation
results.rationale: Generic rubric-based LLM evaluation unification; no Ifá/Odu/palm-nut, no 8-bit binary signature mapping to 256-verse corpus, no babalawo-interpreter; lacks divinatory protocol and ritual repair plan.

results.name: Rubric-Based Evals LLM-Judge 2026
results.rationale: Overview of rubric-based LLM-judge evaluations; no binary signature derivation, no 16x16 compositional Odu tree, no palm-nut sampling cohort; only superficial rubric/judge overlap.

results.name: Rubric-Grounded RL Structured Judge Rewards
results.rationale: RL-driven rubric judge reward shaping; no 8-axis binary collapse, no pre-built 256-entry verse corpus lookup, no ritual repair plan; mechanism diverges substantially.

results.name: LLM-Rubric Multidimensional Calibrated
results.rationale: Multidimensional calibrated rubric scoring shares the multi-axis idea but lacks binary signature, 256-corpus retrieval, palm-nut cohort protocol, and babalawo-style interpreter LLM.

results.name: RRD Recursive Rubric Decomposition
results.rationale: Recursive rubric decomposition resembles the 16x16 main-sub Odu tree conceptually but does not encode binary axis thresholding, fixed 256-entry corpus lookup, or divinatory repair-plan formulation.

results.name: Agent-as-a-Judge Evaluation Survey
results.rationale: Survey of agent-as-judge approaches; no Ifá analogy, no binary-signature-to-corpus mapping, no 8-round palm-nut sampling, and no compositional 16x16 rubric tree.

results.name: Judgment Document Agentic Legal Rubric
results.rationale: Legal-domain agentic rubric evaluator; no binary signature, no 256-verse corpus, no palm-nut cohort, no babalawo-style interpreter; mechanism mismatch.

results.name: Judging the Judges LLM Relevance
results.rationale: Meta-evaluation of LLM judges; no Ifá/Odu structure, no 8-bit signature, no fixed corpus, no ritual repair plan; lacks the compositional decomposition and binary thresholding mechanism.

decision_rationale: All 8 results are generic rubric/judge-based LLM evaluation works lacking the candidate's distinctive 8-bit Ifá signature + 256-verse corpus + palm-nut 16-judge cohort + babalawo-interpreter + 16x16 compositional Odu tree. NOTE: primary scored 8 hits at sem 0.74-0.92 / func 0.74-0.92 on Autorubric + RRD + LLM-Rubric adjacencies; verdict-level disagreement flagged (Pattern E).
